sanskrit:
ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः।
सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत्॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaṅkhāśca bheryaśca paṇavānakagomukhāḥ / sahasaivābhyahanyanta sa śabdastumulo’bhavat //
literal_meaning: Then conch-shells, kettledrums, cymbals, trumpets and cow-horns were suddenly struck all at once, and the sound became tumultuous.
contextual_meaning: The battlefield’s sudden, overwhelming cacophony mirrors the inner explosion that occurs when latent conflict becomes unavoidable: every defense mechanism, identity narrative, and fear is triggered at once, plunging the psyche into sensory overload and anticipatory dread. Arjuna’s external world now screams the way his nervous system is about to scream—an uncontainable surge that signals the collapse of old coping structures and the terrifying threshold of transformation.
psychological_state: A person whose inner alarm system has just gone from zero to maximum volume, flooding them with the certainty that everything they rely on for safety is about to shatter.
trigger_scenario: Standing in the hospital corridor when the code-blue alarm suddenly blares for your child, or receiving a group text that your company is folding today and everyone must clear their desks by noon.
anchor_text: When inner noise drowns every story, stand still; the true battle is hearing what remains.
